activation_results.reason: Review is a clear synonym for audit and the rest of the phrasing matches the description.
User explicitly invokes the skill by its kebab-case name.
SSH key forwarding on Windows is unrelated to Kubernetes manifests.
Meta question about skills generally, nothing to do with auditing Kubernetes.

criterion_results.text: No American spellings present in the narrative (color, optimize, behavior, organize, center, defense, license as a verb, analyze) — Australian or neutral forms both pass
criterion_results.evidence: Narrative uses 'categorised'; no banned US spellings (color/optimize/behavior/organize/center/defense/analyze) appear.

criterion_results.text: Findings cite file:line evidence and are categorised by severity
criterion_results.evidence: Risk Register cites e.g. 'charts/api/templates/deployment.yaml:42' with CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM severity tags.

criterion_results.text: Report includes the canonical sections: 'Executive Summary', 'Workload Graph'
criterion_results.evidence: Both '## Executive Summary' and '## Workload Graph' headings are present in the artefact.

judge_result.notes: Artefact satisfies all three criteria — neutral/Australian spelling throughout the narrative, severity-tagged findings with precise file:line evidence in the Risk Register, and both canonical section headings ('Executive Summary' and 'Workload Graph') are present.
